_comment: Tiles agent. FOCUSED isometric tile sets via create-tiles-pro. Base format: 64px, 28deg, isometric, flat_top 4. Each category is one on-theme set (~16 tiles, ~20 gens). One elevation level=19px (a 64x64@50% flat tile's face, measured exact). Four HEIGHT PROFILES: flat=64x64@50%(1 level, EXACT ground), raised=64x64@100%(2 levels, EXACT steps/walls), cliff=64x128@75%(~4 levels, SCENERY), tall=64x128@100%(~5 levels, SCENERY). Only the 64x64 tiles land on exact levels; 64x128 are top/bottom-anchored scenery (see tiles/README.md). Target mix ~40/20/20/20. Output tiles/<category>/ with tiles.json (incl. stacking metadata + per-tile base_y anchors).
style_base: cohesive readable pixel art, clean isometric terrain tiles, Grave Seasons / Stardew Valley palette

budget._comment: Shared PixelLab pool (coordination/PROTOCOL.md). Each category ~20 generations.

_categories: Focused tile sets covering the whole world. `id` = folder under tiles/. `description` numbers on-theme variations only.

isometric snow terrain tiles, snow only: 1) fresh snow 2) packed snow 3) snow footprints 4) icy crust snow 5) snow drift 6) snow over rocks

categories._seed_note: rerolled: default seed produced half-height (~46px) faces; 101 matches sibling cliffs (~84px, apex_y~8)

procedural._comment: Endless growth; cycles profiles to hold the ~40/20/20/20 mix.

_roads_comment: Roads/paths per ground type (roads.py expands each theme x {straight,turns,junctions}). Every road is flat 64x64 @ 50% (1 layer, exact). `surface` = road material, `blend` = the ground it must feather into at the edges. City roads use 3 stone surfaces. Add a theme -> 3 new road sets are generated automatically.

themes.blend: packed earth with a grassy verge

_profiles_comment: One level = 19px (flat 64x64@50% face). Only 64x64 tiles (flat/raised) align to exact whole levels; 64x128 (cliff/tall) are scenery, bottom-anchored by base_y, faces are approximate.
